question_title: Which film will win Best Picture at the 98th Academy Awards?
summary: The 98th Academy Awards (March 15, 2026) Best Picture race is dominated by "One Battle After Another" (Paul Thomas Anderson), which falls under the "Other" category. OBAA has swept nearly every major precursor: Critics Choice, Golden Globes, DGA, BAFTA, and PGA awards. However, Sinners (Ryan Coogler) won the SAG ensemble award on March 1 during active Oscar final voting, which historically has signaled some Best Picture upsets (CODA, Crash, Spotlight). Sinners also holds a record 16 nominations. Pre-SAG betting markets had OBAA at 74-77%. Post-SAG, a modest 5-point shift toward Sinners is warranted. Frankenstein and Bugonia are extreme long shots ranked 6th and 9th respectively.

factors.description: One Battle After Another swept DGA+PGA+BAFTA+Critics Choice+Golden Globes - historically this near-complete precursor sweep is an extremely strong predictor of Best Picture
Pre-SAG betting markets (Kalshi 74%, Gold Derby 77%) strongly favor One Battle After Another, providing a strong calibration anchor
Hamnet (~5-8%), Marty Supreme (~3%), and other nominees add probability mass to Other beyond just OBAA
SAG/Oscar BP overlap is only ~48% (15 of 31 years), and OBAA's DGA+PGA+BAFTA triple lock remains historically near-decisive, limiting SAG's upset predictive power

Sinners won SAG ensemble on March 1 during active Oscar voting (Feb 26-Mar 5), historically predicting upsets (CODA, Crash, Shakespeare in Love, Spotlight). Timing during voting amplifies significance.
Sinners holds record 16 Oscar nominations indicating broad branch support across the Academy
Feinberg (THR's top Oscar prognosticator) picked Sinners post-SAG - one respected contrarian voice against broader consensus
Frankenstein ranked 6th by IndieWire with long odds on prediction markets - very unlikely Best Picture winner
Bugonia ranked 9th by IndieWire with <1% implied probability - extremely unlikely to win

reasoning:
**Final Forecast: 98th Academy Awards Best Picture**

| Option | Probability |
|--------|------------|
| **Other** | **75%** |
| **Sinners** | **20%** |
| **Frankenstein** | **3%** |
| **Bugonia** | **2%** |

**Key reasoning:**

The frontrunner "One Battle After Another" (Paul Thomas Anderson) falls under **Other** and has swept nearly every major precursor award (Critics Choice, Golden Globes, DGA, BAFTA, PGA). Pre-SAG betting markets priced it at 74-77%. This precursor sweep is historically one of the strongest predictors of Best Picture.

**Sinners** gets 20% reflecting a modest boost from its SAG ensemble win yesterday (March 1) during active Oscar final voting, its record 16 nominations, and the historical pattern where SAG ensemble victories sometimes presage Best Picture upsets. However, the DGA+PGA+BAFTA triple lock for OBAA significantly limits the SAG signal's predictive power.

**Frankenstein** and **Bugonia** are extreme long shots at 3% and 2% respectively, consistent with their bottom-half rankings among nominees.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option that matches the Best Picture winner listed on the official Oscars ceremony page for 2026. The primary source is https://www.oscars.org/oscars/ceremonies/2026
fine_print: If the Oscars page is unavailable, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions while prioritizing official Academy sources. Candidate Sources: Academy Awards database https://awardsdatabase.oscars.org
